I found comprehensive information about the Monta Partner API documentation and endpoints. Here's what I discovered:

## Overview

The Partner API enables you to develop your own use cases around Monta-managed charge points, such as presenting them within your own solutions, starting/stopping charges, or importing information into your CRM tool.

## Key Capabilities

The Partner API gives you full control of tariffs that reflect real-world energy costs, and developers get straightforward access to live charging data, charge point control, and transaction syncing.

Webhooks push updates straight into your system, powering everything from live charge status updates to automated workflows, with OCPP webhooks being especially powerful for accessing raw charging events.

## API Endpoints

You can utilize the GET /charge-points or GET /sites APIs to retrieve your charge points, and it is recommended to utilize the webhooks system to retrieve updates to your charge points.

You can use GET /charges/{chargeId} if you know the charge ID, or GET /charges?partnerExternalId={yourPartnerExternalId} if you set a partnerExternalId when starting a charge.

There is also an endpoint at https://partner-api.monta.com/api/v1/charge-point-integrations that will create or update an existing integration.

## Access Requirements

Full API Access is available for partners on the Enterprise tier with a commitment of 500+ charge points.

To get access to the Partner API, you need to get in touch with your Monta representative to discuss your use case and establish a commercial agreement, after which the technical team will guide you through the integration process, and you can create Partner API credentials in Monta Hub.

The official documentation is available at **https://docs.partner-api.monta.com/**

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Monta Partner API authentication:

## OAuth2 Token Endpoint

The token endpoint is: `https://partner-api.monta.com/api/v1/auth/token`

## Authentication Flow

Use your clientId and clientSecret to request a short-lived access token from the authentication endpoint. Include the returned accessToken in the Authorization header as a Bearer token for all subsequent API requests.

## Credentials

If you have access to Monta Partner API you can find your credentials in the Monta Hub. Once approved, you'll be provisioned with a clientId and clientSecret.

## Security Note

Your clientId and clientSecret should not be part of any frontend (app, web, …) - a server-to-server integration is expected.

For complete request and response details, the Monta documentation references their full Authentication API reference page at https://docs.partner-api.monta.com/reference/ref-authentication

content.text:
[View your fixed dashboard](computer:///sessions/zen-dreamy-mayer/mnt/outputs/monta-dashboard.html)

Here's exactly what was wrong and what I fixed by inspecting the live API responses:

**Every field name was wrong.** The Monta API uses different names than what the original code guessed:

| Bug | Old (broken) | Fixed |
|---|---|---|
| kWh field | `kwh` / `energyKwh` / `energy` | `consumedKwh` |
| Status field | `status` | `state` |
| Coordinates | guessing `cp.lat/lng` | `location.coordinates.latitude/longitude` |
| Max power | `maxPower` | `maxKW` |
| Pagination param | `pageSize` (ignored by API → 10/page) | `perPage` (returns up to 100/page) |
| Pagination stop | `data.total` | `meta.totalPageCount` |
| Date filtering | Sent params to API (silently ignored) | Removed — now fetches all 3,086 sessions and filters client-side |

You have **108 charge points** and **3,086 charge sessions** — the old code was only ever seeing the first 10 of each. The dashboard now fetches everything (shows a live counter as it pages through), and switching between time periods (7 days / 30 days / all time) is instant since the data is already in memory.

content.text:
The dashboard is ready. Here's what's in the complete rebuild:

**5 tabs, all powered by live Monta API data:**

**Overview** — Total sessions, kWh, revenue, active chargers; daily energy & session charts; charger status donut; recent sessions table.

**Utilization** — kWh/day, sessions/day, hours charging/day, utilization %; site ranking table with color-coded utilization bars (green/yellow/red); horizontal bar charts for top sites; daily trend lines to spot patterns for replication decisions.

**Profitability** — Uses your cost model ($0.16/kWh electricity, $0.03/kWh rev share, $20/mo/charger ops). Portfolio KPIs, site-level profit table with margins, per-charger breakdown (top 30 by revenue), monthly profit trend bar chart, and a cost breakdown donut.

**Revenue Payout** — Gross revenue, owner payout, electricity cost, ops cost, and net-to-you KPIs; monthly revenue vs costs line chart; revenue distribution donut; site-level payout table.

**Map & Heat Map** — Fixed using `unpkg.com` CDN for Leaflet.heat; markers colored by charger state; heat intensity weighted by kWh dispensed; radius/intensity sliders; sidebar sorted by sessions with click-to-zoom.

All cost inputs (electricity rate, rev share, ops cost) are editable live in the control bar — change them and all three financial tabs update instantly. The date range filter works client-side across all tabs.

content.text:
Fixed. The issue was that the Monta API doesn't return a `durationSeconds` or `duration` field at all — those were guesses that always resolved to zero. I inspected a live session and confirmed it only provides timestamps (`startedAt`, `stoppedAt`, `completedAt`, etc.).

The fix adds a `getDurS()` helper that calculates duration in seconds from `stoppedAt - startedAt` (with fallbacks to `completedAt` and `releasedAt`), and all three places in the dashboard that track charging hours now use it. This means your Utilization tab's "Hrs Charging/Day", "Utilization %", and the session duration column in Overview will all show accurate values.

[View updated dashboard](computer:///sessions/zen-dreamy-mayer/mnt/outputs/monta-dashboard.html)

Now here's exactly what to do — just copy and paste these into your terminal (replace `YOUR-REPO-URL` with the URL from the new repo you create on GitHub):

---

**Step 1 — Create the GitHub repo**

Go to [github.com/new](https://github.com/new), name it something like `monta-dashboard`, set it to **Private**, and click *Create repository*. Don't add a README.

---

**Step 2 — Push from your folder**

Open Terminal, `cd` to the folder you selected in Cowork (your outputs folder), then run:

```bash
git init
git add .
git commit -m "Initial commit: Monta EV dashboard"
git branch -M main
git remote add origin YOUR-REPO-URL
git push -u origin main
```

---

**Step 3 — Deploy on Vercel**

1. Go to [vercel.com](https://vercel.com) → *Add New Project*
2. Import your GitHub repo
3. Leave all settings as default — Vercel will detect it as a static site automatically
4. Click *Deploy*

The `vercel.json` I added routes all traffic to `monta-dashboard.html`, so your URL (e.g. `monta-dashboard.vercel.app`) will load the dashboard directly. Takes about 30 seconds to deploy.
